title: scurrula haenkeana
intro: Scurrula haenkeana, also known as the parasitic mistletoe, is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae, which is native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Scurrula haenkeana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties.
